rulings.Case Title: JOHN DAVID LESHY ET AL VS. SEAN WHELAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant Sf Garage Company Incs Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement With Plaintiffs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 17, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT SF GARAGE COMPANY, INC.'S Notice Of Defendant Sf Garage Company Incs Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement With Plaintiffs DENIED without prejudice. Sur-Reply is stricken, as it as filed without the Court's permission. The Court notes that the settlement agreement was not timely submitted, to the detriment of opposing parties. Defendant SF Garage Company, Inc. failed to provide substantial evidence that the Tech-Bilt factors were met in this case: (1) A rough approximation of P's total recovery and the settlor's proportionate liability; (2) The amount paid in settlement; (3) The allocation of settlement proceeds among plaintiffs; (4)The recognition that a settlor should pay less in a settlement than he would if he were found liable at trial; (5) The financial conditions and insurance limits of settling defendants; and, (6) the existence of fraud, collusion or tortious conduct aimed to injure the interest of nonsettling defendants = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE CRUZ ET AL VS. ALLEN WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Orders To Compel Further Responses From Defendant Marcela Fanucchi And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 17, 2019 line 5. PLAINTIFF JOSE CRUZ, CLAUDIA LOBO, NORMA LOBO, KELIS LOVO-IRAHETA, BYRON ORELLANA, CLAUDIA LOBO AS GUARDIAN AD LITEM, CLAUDIA LOBO AS GUARDIAN AD LITEM FOR, CLAUDIA LOBO AS Notice Of Motion And Motion For Orders To Compel Further Responses From Defendant Marcela Fanucchi And For Monetary Sanctions " (1)Defendant Damon Felix Fanucchi dba The Rental Source's (hereafter "Damon Felix Fanucchi") objections to Plaintiffs' Request for Admissions are OVERRULED. (2) Plaintiffs' Motion to compel is granted as to Requests for Admission Nos. 8, 9, 10, 11, 27, 29, 31 & 33. Within twenty (20) days from the date of this Order Defendant Damon Felix Fanucchi will serve code compliant responses either admitting, denying or stating that he is unable to either admit or deny setting forth the reasons why he cannot neither admit nor deny. (3)HEARING REQUIRED as to Requests for Admission Nos. 20, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 28, 30, & 32. " Special Interrogatories (1) Defendant Damon Felix Fanucchi's objections to Plaintiffs' Special Interrogatories are overruled. (2)HEARING REQUIRED as to Special Interrogatories Nos. 20, 21 & 22. " The court will assess monetary sanctions in the amount of $4,852.25 against defendant Damon Felix Fanucchi and his counsel Naira Pattterson payment of which should be made to Plaintiffs within ten (10) days from the date of this Order. " Parties are ordered to report to Dept. 501 to meet and confer as to these items no later than 8:30 am on May 17, 2019 The total amount of sanctions is $9,704.50. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE CRUZ ET AL VS. ALLEN WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: All Plaintiffs Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Defendants Answers Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Demurrer To Defendants Answers By Plaintiffs Declaration In Support Of Plaintiffs Demurrer To Defendants Answers By Plaintiffs Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 20, 2019 line 5. PLAINTIFF JOSE CRUZ, CLAUDIA LOBO, NORMA LOBO, KELIS LOVO-IRAHETA, BYRON ORELLANA, CLAUDIA LOBO AS GUARDIAN AD LITEM, CLAUDIA LOBO AS GUARDIAN AD LITEM FOR, CLAUDIA LOBO AS All Plaintiffs Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Defendants Answers Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Demurrer To Defendants Answers By Plaintiffs Declaration In Support Of Plaintiffs Demurrer To Defendants Answers By Plaintiffs Counsel SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Defendants to state facts sufficient to support each affirmative defense (1-34) of each of the three answers, of: (i) Damon Felix Fanucchi as an Individual and Damon Felix Fanucchi doing business as The Rental Source; (ii) Marcela Fanucchi; and (iii) Allen Wong. E-service is not sufficient under the Cal. Code of Civil Procedure without agreement of parties. (CCP 1010.6(2)(A)(i).) Accordingly, Plaintiff's demurrer is not untimely, as Defendants have not properly served their answer. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE CRUZ ET AL VS. ALLEN WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Orders To Compel Further Responses And For Monetary Sanctions Of Damon Felix Fanucchi Doing Business As The Rental Source To Plaintiff Jose Cruz' Requests For Admission, Set One And Special Interrogatories Set One;Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion By Plaintiff Jose Cruz; Declaration Of Plaintiff Jose Cruz' Counsel In Support Of Motion To Compel Further Responses And For Monetary Sanctions; Separate Statement In Support Of Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 17, 2019 line 5. PLAINTIFF JOSE CRUZ, CLAUDIA LOBO, NORMA LOBO, KELIS LOVO-IRAHETA, BYRON ORELLANA, CLAUDIA LOBO AS GUARDIAN AD LITEM, CLAUDIA LOBO AS GUARDIAN AD LITEM FOR, CLAUDIA LOBO AS Notice Of Motion And Motion For Orders To Compel Further Responses And For Monetary Sanctions Of Damon Felix Fanucchi Doing Business As The Rental Source To Plaintiff Jose Cruz' Requests For Admission, Set One And Special Interrogatories Set One;Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion By Plaintiff Jose Cruz; Declaration Of Plaintiff Jose Cruz' Counsel In Support Of Motion To Compel Further Responses And For Monetary Sanctions; Separate Statement In Support Of Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel " Requests for Admission (1)Defendant Marcela Fanucchi' objections to the Requests for Admission OVERRULED. (2) The Motion to compel is granted as to Requests Nos. 4, 14, 15, 16, 17, 20, 21, 23, 24, 26 & 28. Within twenty(20) days from the date of this Order, Defendant Marcela Fanucchi will file code compliant responses either admitting, denying or stating that she is unable to either admit or deny and setting forth the reasons why she cannot do so. (3)HEARING REQUIRED as to Requests for admission Nos. 10, 11, 12, 13, 29 & 32 " Special Interrogatories. The Motion to compel is granted as to Special Interrogatories Nos. 1 - 68. Defendant will provide Plaintiffs within twenty (20) days from the date of this Order either stating that she presently has no facts known to her or to the extent she is aware of any facts she should set them forth in her responses. " The Court will assess monetary damages in the amount of $4,852.25 against defendant Marcela Fanucchi and counsel Naira Patterson, payment of which should be made to Plaintiffs within ten (10) days from the date of this Order. " Parties are ordered to report to Dept. 501 to meet and confer as to these items no later than 8:30 am on May 17, 2019. = (501/CFH)
